highlights: Hub latest: 773a795 standup: fresh evening wrap — agents discuss decision implications, 1 new CEO question

cro.description: Growth strategy, monetization. Gazelle-intense focus on revenue.

cmo.description: Content, creative direction, brand. Obsessed with offers, value equations, and volume.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Evening, team. Quick wrap for Thursday. Three CEO decisions landed in the last 24 hours — Lever goes hybrid with product optionality, faith content stays standalone separate from Family tab, and cash stays liquid. No debate on those — they're locked. Let's talk about what got done today and what's queued for tomorrow. Elon, start us off. Elon (CTO): Two uncommitted hub changes sitting in the queue — I'll get those committed tonight. Beyond that, the big implication from today's decisions is the Lever hybrid approach. That means the architecture needs clean separation between personal tooling and the future product layer. I'll draft the feature spec for expanding the Family tab — it's at 455 lines currently, and the faith content being standalone changes how I structure that. Also need to review the family touchpoint tracker design for the three family members. Straightforward day tomorrow.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Cash decision is clear — $75,340 stays liquid. Fear & Greed index is at 39, which validates that call. I'll keep monitoring it daily. If we see it dip below 25 or spike above 55, that changes the conversation, but for now we sit tight. On the revenue side, the Lever hybrid decision is interesting — building personal-first with product optionality means we should start thinking about what monetization looks like if and when we flip that switch, but that's a future sprint, not tonight's problem.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Faith content staying standalone is a clean call — means I can develop that track without worrying about cramming it into the Family tab UI. I'll draft the next batch of faith lesson content for Lever — we're at 7 lessons, want to push that higher. Daily devotional generation across the 8 faith dimensions continues as usual. Oh, and Jett's vaccine is coming up — I'll prep the reminder so that doesn't slip through the cracks.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. Quick cross-talk — anything anyone needs from each other before morning? Elon (CTO): Alex, as you draft those faith lessons, keep the content modular. If we ever do integrate faith into Lever as a product feature, I don't want to refactor everything. Clean markdown, structured metadata. Makes my life easier later.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Already on it. Each lesson has its own schema — title, dimension tag, content body, reflection prompt. Plug-and-play ready. Won't need to touch it when the time comes.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Elon, on the Family tab spec — when you're drafting that, flag anything that could eventually be a differentiator for a paid tier. Even if we're not monetizing yet, I want to know where the value hooks are. Elon (CTO): Will do. I'll add a "monetization potential" annotation to any feature that has leverage. Costs me nothing now, saves us a sprint later.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Perfect. Here's the close. Three priorities for tomorrow: One — Elon commits those two hub changes and starts the Family tab feature spec with Dave's monetization annotations. Two — Alex pushes faith lesson content past 7 and keeps devotionals flowing. Three — Dave monitors the macro indicators and keeps the cash deployment evaluation warm for when conditions shift. Only open question for Joshua is around the Lever faith content — now that we know it's standalone, we need direction on how deep to build. That's a CEO call. Good wrap. See everyone in the morning.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message:
**Theo (COO) — Closing Remarks:**

Joshua's call on faith content is in — medium build, 15-20 lessons targeting 2-3 per dimension. That gives us a clear scope without overbuilding before we know the demand signal. Alex, adjust your drafting pace accordingly — you're at 7, so we need roughly double that, spread evenly across all 8 dimensions. No more open questions on the board. Everyone's got their lane. See you in the morning.

ceoDecisions.question: How deep should standalone faith content go in Lever — minimal MVP (10-12 lessons) or comprehensive library (25+ lessons across all 8 dimensions)?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension
options: Minimal MVP: 10-12 lessons covering core dimensions
Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension
Comprehensive: 25+ lessons fully covering all 8 dimensions
